site.title: NRL TipBot Briefing 2026

site.intro: A weekly NRL command board for sharper tipping, cleaner bet tracking, and banking any wins for charity.

meta.description: Codex-managed NRL TipBot dashboard for Round 17 tips, Round 16 result notes, and weekly NRL team news.

featurePanels.title: Round Read
items: Round 17 is now in result-tracking mode rather than preview mode.
Rabbitohs over Eels and Bulldogs over Titans are verified correct from the clean public score table.
Panthers over Cowboys and Storm over Sea Eagles are logged as user-reported misses, with exact scores still pending clean cross-check.
Do not publish a final Round 17 tip count until Broncos v Roosters, Dolphins v Warriors and the Sunday games are cleanly checked.

featurePanels.title: Risk Flags
items: The Panthers miss hurt most because it was the top-ranked raw tip.
The Storm miss was flagged as a dangerous 4 Pines spot, but it still counts as a miss.
External bet-slip losses are not charity-ledger losses unless a stake, price and user-confirmed charity play were recorded before kickoff.
Exact scores for the Panthers and Storm misses need a clean public source before finalising the scoreboard.

featurePanels.title: Weekly Focus
items: Protect the positive Mark Hughes Foundation ledger at +$0.53.
Record misses honestly and avoid chasing the round.
Keep Round 17 user bet-slip outcomes separate from the Mark Hughes Foundation ledger.
Round 17 had no locked charity bet, so no ledger movement is recorded.

featurePanels.title: Watchlist
items: Broncos v Roosters needs a clean score cross-check before marking the tip.
Dolphins v Warriors needs a clean score cross-check before marking the tip.
Raiders v Dragons and Knights v Wests Tigers remain Sunday pending.
Do not convert any remaining lean into a chase bet.

betCard.title: Round 17 Charity Bet
betCard.kickoff: Round runs Thursday 25 June to Sunday 28 June 2026
why: The ledger is positive at +$0.53 and was protected because no Round 17 charity bet was locked.
User selected the TipBot card externally, and the Panthers and Storm legs have already cost the slip.
Those external losses are recorded as betting reality, not as charity-ledger movement.
risks: Chasing after two misses would turn a controlled tipping board into an undisciplined betting board.
The remaining games should be tracked, not used to rescue the round.
Any future charity play still needs recorded odds, stake, match, and explicit confirmation before kickoff.

sections.title: Situation Report
sections.heading: Round 17 has moved from preview to damage-control tracking
lines: As of Saturday, 27 June 2026, Round 17 result tracking has started.
Verified correct tips: Rabbitohs over Eels and Bulldogs over Titans.
Logged misses from the user's bet slip: Panthers over Cowboys and Storm over Sea Eagles.
The charity ledger remains 3-2 with +$0.53 settled profit because no Round 17 charity bet was locked.

sections.title: Bet Analysis
sections.heading: Do not let a losing slip rewrite the charity ledger
lines: The Mark Hughes Foundation ledger is still 3-2 with $31.50 invested, $32.03 returned and +$0.53 settled profit.
The user's external card has taken damage through Panthers and Storm, but those were not locked charity bets.
The correct response is to record the misses and stop chasing.
No Round 17 charity result should be added without a pre-recorded stake, price and explicit charity-bet confirmation.

sections.title: Round 17 Fixtures
sections.heading: Round 17 fixture board
lines: Eels v Rabbitohs - Thursday 25 June, 7:50pm at CommBank Stadium.
Titans v Bulldogs - Friday 26 June, 6:00pm at Cbus Super Stadium.
Broncos v Roosters - Friday 26 June, 8:00pm at Suncorp Stadium.
Dolphins v Warriors - Saturday 27 June, 3:00pm at Suncorp Stadium.
Cowboys v Panthers - Saturday 27 June, 5:30pm at Queensland Country Bank Stadium.
Sea Eagles v Storm - Saturday 27 June, 7:35pm at 4 Pines Park.
Raiders v Dragons - Sunday 28 June, 2:00pm at GIO Stadium.
Knights v Wests Tigers - Sunday 28 June, 4:05pm at McDonald Jones Stadium.
Sharks have the bye.

sections.title: Form Radar
sections.heading: What the misses taught the board
lines: Panthers was over-weighted as a rebound spot and should be marked as the round's worst read.
Storm was correctly flagged as a dangerous away favourite spot, but still should not have stayed on the ticket without more price edge.
Bulldogs handled the Titans trap better than expected.
Rabbitohs rewarded the low-confidence lean, but it was still not a bet-building anchor.

sections.title: Intel Notes
sections.heading: The Round 17 team-list details driving the card
lines: Jackson Ford is out 10-12 weeks with a pectoral injury, weakening a Warriors side already without Roger Tuivasa-Sheck.
Penrith name Dylan Edwards, Brian To'o, Nathan Cleary, Isaah Yeo and Liam Martin for the Townsville trip.
The Broncos name Reece Walsh, Kotoni Staggs and Payne Haas, but Ezra Mam is out and Thomas Duffy partners Ben Hunt.
The Dolphins regain Hamiso Tabuai-Fidow, Selwyn Cobbo, Thomas Flegler, Max Plath and Kulikefu Finefeuiaki from the Round 16 rest cycle.
The Tigers name Adam Doueihi and Alex Twal back, but Jock Madden and Patrick Herbert are out after Round 16 injuries.
